notes: Planning-mode eval corpus for three-lane evaluation. Iterate in iteration-N directories.

evals.prompt: Design architecture for a greenfield B2B marketplace API. Requirements: buyer/seller accounts, catalog, checkout, Stripe payments, order webhooks, and admin analytics. Constraints: launch in 8 weeks, small team (4 engineers), SOC2 roadmap, and no multi-region for v1.
evals.expected_output: Clear bounded services/containers, payment workflow, ownership of order/payment state, and explicit unknowns for SOC2 roadmap scope.
assertions: Separates API boundary from payment provider integration boundary.
Defines a single system of record for order and payment entities.
Includes webhook handling sequence with idempotency concerns.
Captures launch constraints as plan evidence and confidence levels.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Use an async queue between checkout and fulfillment; remove direct sync fulfillment calls.
expected_revision_assertions: Checkout-to-fulfillment path is async in revised flow.
Existing unchanged IDs are preserved.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Clarify what data is authoritative in payments vs orders and call out reconciliation.
expected_revision_assertions: System-of-record boundaries for orders vs payments are explicit.
Reconciliation flow is represented in sequence or notes.

evals.prompt: Design architecture for a notification platform handling email, SMS, and push with 5M events/day. Requirements: templates, scheduling, retries, dead-letter queue, customer segmentation, and observability. Constraint: providers can fail and must be swappable.
evals.expected_output: Event-driven architecture with queue/topic boundaries, retry/DLQ ownership, provider abstraction, and failure isolation.
assertions: Shows event ingestion, processing, and retry/DLQ paths.
Avoids assigning provider adapters as core system-of-record owners.
Includes observability path for delivery outcomes.
Captures provider-swappability as tradeoff/evidence.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Separate template rendering from channel delivery workers.
expected_revision_assertions: Template rendering boundary separated from delivery workers.
Sequence updated without introducing dangling IDs.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Add explicit backpressure and retry budget decisions.
expected_revision_assertions: Backpressure strategy documented in architecture rationale.
Retry budget assumptions are explicit.

evals.prompt: Design a patient intake platform for clinics. Requirements: e-sign forms, PHI storage, role-based access, audit trails, and exports to EHR systems. Constraints: HIPAA-like controls from day one, encryption at rest/in transit, least privilege.
evals.expected_output: Architecture with explicit PHI boundaries, access-control components, audit logging, and clear compliance assumptions/unknowns.
assertions: Identifies PHI system-of-record boundaries and sensitive data flows.
Includes audit trail ownership and immutability considerations.
Shows RBAC/authn/authz boundaries separately from business logic.
Captures compliance assumptions and unresolved control decisions.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Split operational logs from compliance audit logs and tighten retention policy ownership.
expected_revision_assertions: Operational logs and compliance audit logs are distinct entities.
Retention policy ownership is explicit.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Call out key management boundary and rotation responsibilities.
expected_revision_assertions: Key management boundary appears in model/views.
Rotation responsibility is attributed in rationale.

evals.prompt: Design architecture for a product analytics MVP for startups. Requirements: ingest events, basic dashboards, cohort reports, and CSV export. Constraints: strict monthly infra budget under $2k, one backend engineer maintaining the system.
evals.expected_output: Cost-aware architecture with minimal moving parts, explicit cost tradeoffs, and deferred complexity recorded as assumptions.
assertions: Avoids unnecessary service sprawl given budget constraint.
Documents tradeoffs between managed services vs self-hosting.
Defines authoritative ownership for event and aggregate datasets.
Records deferred capabilities as explicit unknowns or out-of-scope.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Prefer one deployable backend + managed warehouse for v1; remove speculative streaming service.
expected_revision_assertions: Streaming service removed or marked deferred.
Managed warehouse role and ownership clarified.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Add explicit operational burden notes for on-call and maintenance overhead.
expected_revision_assertions: Operational burden appears in rationale/tradeoffs.
Architecture choices reflect maintenance constraints.

evals.prompt: Design architecture for a new social app with posting, comments, and feeds. No extra constraints provided.
evals.expected_output: Reasonable initial architecture proposal with clearly labeled assumptions and unknowns instead of fabricated certainty.
assertions: Unknowns are explicitly listed for scale, moderation, and abuse handling.
Confidence levels reflect weak evidence where constraints are missing.
No invented hard requirements appear as confirmed facts.
Provides targeted questions for engineer feedback.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Target 200k DAU in year one and add image uploads with moderation queue.
expected_revision_assertions: Scale assumptions updated with new DAU constraint.
Image moderation queue appears in architecture.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Regulatory requirement: EU user data must stay in EU region.
expected_revision_assertions: Data residency boundary is explicit.
Any multi-region implications are documented as tradeoffs.

evals.prompt: Design architecture for a multi-tenant SaaS project management app with org workspaces, role-based permissions, audit history, and seat-based billing.
evals.expected_output: Tenant boundary strategy, identity and authorization boundaries, billing integration flow, and ownership for tenant metadata.
assertions: Defines tenant boundary approach and key data partitioning decision.
Separates authn/authz from billing workflow responsibilities.
Includes audit history ownership and consistency assumptions.
Captures migration/scale unknowns for tenant growth.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Add requirement for enterprise SSO and SCIM provisioning.
expected_revision_assertions: SSO/SCIM boundaries and dependencies are added.
Existing IDs preserved for unchanged modules.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Clarify whether billing events are source-of-truth in app or provider.
expected_revision_assertions: SoR assignment for billing entities is explicit and non-duplicated.

evals.prompt: Design architecture for a collaborative document editor with realtime presence, comments, and offline edits sync.
evals.expected_output: Clear realtime vs persistence boundaries, conflict-resolution workflow, and explicit unknowns for offline merge strategy.
assertions: Distinguishes ephemeral presence state from authoritative document state.
Models sync/conflict path in sequence workflow.
Avoids implying guaranteed ordering where not supported.
Captures tradeoffs for offline conflict resolution strategy.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Move from naive last-write-wins to CRDT-based merge strategy.
expected_revision_assertions: Conflict-resolution design updated to reflect CRDT strategy.
Associated assumptions and risks updated.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Add document export pipeline for PDF and DOCX.
expected_revision_assertions: Export boundary added without confusing SoR ownership.

evals.prompt: Design architecture for an internal FinOps recommendation engine that ingests cloud billing exports, computes optimization suggestions, and notifies owners weekly. Constraint: must run mostly in batch with predictable spend.
evals.expected_output: Batch-oriented architecture with explicit cost controls, data ownership for billing snapshots, and recommendation workflow.
assertions: Uses batch-first workflow and avoids unnecessary realtime complexity.
Defines ownership for imported billing snapshots and derived recommendations.
Includes notification path and failure handling.
Tradeoffs explain cost predictability decisions.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Add support for ad-hoc on-demand recommendation runs with rate limits.
expected_revision_assertions: On-demand path added with guardrails and capacity assumptions.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Need explainability metadata for each recommendation.
expected_revision_assertions: Explainability data ownership and flow are explicit.

evals.prompt: Design architecture for user-generated content moderation with policy rules, appeal workflows, and regulator reporting exports.
evals.expected_output: Policy decision boundaries, moderation queue workflows, and reporting/audit ownership with compliance evidence.
assertions: Separates policy rules service from moderation execution pipeline.
Models appeal workflow as first-class sequence path.
Defines ownership for moderation decisions and audit evidence.
Captures regulator reporting assumptions and unknowns.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Add human-in-the-loop review stage for high-risk categories.
expected_revision_assertions: Human review stage appears in workflow and state transitions.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Need immutable evidence retention for overturned decisions.
expected_revision_assertions: Evidence retention boundary and SoR clarified for appeals outcomes.

evals.prompt: Design architecture for an internal developer platform with service catalog, golden templates, and deploy workflow integration with existing CI systems.
evals.expected_output: Control-plane architecture with clear boundaries between catalog metadata, template generation, and deployment orchestration.
assertions: Clearly separates control-plane APIs from execution workers.
Defines ownership for service metadata and template artifacts.
Includes integration boundaries to existing CI systems.
Captures extensibility assumptions and plugin boundary unknowns.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Support policy-as-code checks before deployment orchestration.
expected_revision_assertions: Policy-as-code stage added with clear fail/allow behavior.

engineer_feedback_rounds.feedback: Clarify rollback ownership and state transitions after failed deploys.
expected_revision_assertions: Rollback workflow and ownership explicitly modeled.
